title: No CONTRIBUTING.md guide for open source contributors
description:
**Ease of Contribution**

Standard open source practice includes a CONTRIBUTING.md explaining:
1. How to set up the development environment
2. Code style expectations (ruff config exists but isn't documented)
3. How to run tests
4. PR process and review expectations
5. Issue triage labels/priorities
6. Architecture overview for newcomers

The README has basic setup commands but a dedicated CONTRIBUTING.md would:
- Be discoverable (GitHub surfaces it in the PR template)
- Separate contributor docs from user docs
- Set expectations about code quality

Should include:
- `pip install -e '.[dev]'` workflow
- Pre-commit hooks recommendation
- mypy --strict requirement
- Test coverage expectations

Files: CONTRIBUTING.md (new)
